metadata.verified_against: ANSI/ISA-5.1-2009/2022/2024, cross-referenced with InstruNexus, control.com, Piping Designer, practitioner forums
metadata.purpose: ISA 5.1 tag validation rules for Tag Validator agent, Sparks AI brain, and P&ID extraction. Enables instrumentation-aware validation and trade-level conversations.

isa_succeeding_letters.ordering_rule: In a tag, modifiers come immediately after the letter they modify. Readout functions come before output functions. C (control) must precede V (valve) when both appear.

function_modifiers.description: Modifiers that appear after function letters. These are NOT OCR errors — they are valid ISA designations.

C.note: NOT an OCR error when on Z-type switches

O.note: NOT an OCR error when on Z-type switches

function_modifiers.validation_rule: Double letters HH and LL are valid ISA designations for safety-level alarms. Do NOT flag as typos or duplicates.

loop_composition_templates.description: Standard instrument groupings by process variable. A 'complete' loop has most of these. Missing instruments are informational flags, not errors.

typical_instruments: FE (Flow Element — orifice plate, venturi, etc.)
flow_control.tag_loop_number_rule: All instruments in the loop share the same loop number. TAG LOOP NUMBER uses the primary controller: FE-2131 → FIC-2131

typical_instruments: PG (Pressure Gauge — local, often not in DCS)
ZSC (Position Switch Closed — confirms valve is closed)
ZSO (Position Switch Open — confirms valve is open)
on_off_valve.critical_rule: ZSC and ZSO are a MATCHED PAIR — both are valid, both should exist. Do NOT flag as duplicates or OCR errors.
on_off_valve.note: Some loops also include ZI (Position Indicator) or ZT (Position Transmitter) for analog position feedback.

safety_shutdown.description: Emergency Shutdown / Safety Instrumented System (SIS) loop
typical_instruments: SDV (Shutdown Valve — alias for XV in safety context)
safety_shutdown.note: Safety loops often have redundant instruments (2oo3 voting). Multiple PSH tags in the same loop is valid.

motor_control.note: HS tags often come in pairs (HS-102A start, HS-102B stop) or with letter suffixes. This is NOT duplication.

tag_suffix_rules.description: Rules for the suffix portion of instrument tags (the number after the loop number)
rules: Suffix indicates the instance number of that instrument type in the loop
Gap in suffix sequence (01, 03 — missing 02) MAY indicate a removed or missed instrument, but is informational only
Letter suffixes (A, B) on non-Z instruments usually indicate physical location or function variant: HS-102A (start), HS-102B (stop)
Single-digit suffixes (just -1 instead of -01) are valid in some project conventions
All instruments on the same P&ID drawing typically share the same prefix (area code)
Prefix format varies by project: 201C, 509A, 3, 5, LO, etc.
A tag with a DIFFERENT prefix than all others on the same drawing is suspicious — could be OCR error OR a cross-referenced instrument from another area
Numeric-only prefixes (3, 5) are valid — these are short area codes
Alphanumeric prefixes (201C, 509A, LO) are common in larger facilities

ocr_confusion_in_context.description: OCR errors that are specific to instrument tags — where the ISA context helps disambiguate

patterns.rule: S is the correct letter (Switch). 5 is an OCR misread. In ISA context, there is no function letter 5.
S is the correct letter (Switch), O is the correct modifier (Open). 50 would make no sense as ISA function letters.
V = Valve. U is not a standard ISA succeeding letter. XU is almost certainly XV with OCR confusion.
I = Indicator. 1 is an OCR misread. In ISA, function letters are always alphabetic.
Same as FIC/F1C. I is Indicator, not digit 1.
HH = High-High (safety alarm). The second H could be misread as M. Double-H is a standard ISA safety designation.
S = Safety. 5 is OCR confusion. PSV = Pressure Safety Valve (relief valve).
V = Valve. U is not standard. HV = Hand Valve.

ocr_confusion_in_context.general_rule: In ISA tag TYPE fields, ALL characters should be letters. Any digit in the TYPE position (between prefix-dash and loop-dash) is almost certainly an OCR error: I→1, S→5, O→0, B→8, Z→2.

tags_that_look_wrong_but_are_correct.description: Common false positives that the agent should NOT flag

examples.explanation: Position Switch Closed and Position Switch Open. Both valid, both needed in XV loops. NOT duplicates.
Double letters HH and LL are safety alarm levels (High-High, Low-Low). NOT typos.
Letter suffixes A/B on Hand Switches indicate Start/Stop or different physical locations. NOT duplicates.
PD = Pressure Differential. D is a modifier, T is Transmitter. Three-letter function codes are valid.

examples.tag: Multiple PSH tags in one loop
examples.explanation: Safety loops often have redundant sensors for voting (2oo3). Multiple pressure switches in one loop = valid.
Some projects use short tags without area prefixes. FE-2131 (no prefix) is valid if the project convention allows it.
LO is a valid area prefix (e.g., Lube Oil system). NOT a partial tag or OCR error.

non_standard_industry_tags.description: Tags that are NOT ISA 5.1 standard but appear on real P&IDs constantly, especially in oil and gas. The system MUST recognize these even though they are not ISA-compliant.
non_standard_industry_tags.principle: ISA 5.1 tags describe FUNCTION (what the instrument does). Industry tags like MOV, SOV, BDV describe CONSTRUCTION (how it's built). Both appear on real drawings.

MOV.isa_equivalent: depends on function (LV, PV, FV, or XV)

CV.isa_equivalent: FCV, PCV, TCV, LCV (with first letter)

non_standard_industry_tags.validation_note: Do NOT flag these as invalid tags. They are real instruments on real drawings. Recognize them and understand what they mean.

zsc_zso_clarification.description: Important distinction for ZSC/ZSO tagging conventions
zsc_zso_clarification.strict_isa: ISA 5.1 Table 4.1 defines H (High) and L (Low) as the standard position modifiers. Strictly speaking, ZSH = Position Switch High (fully open), ZSL = Position Switch Low (fully closed).
zsc_zso_clarification.industry_practice: ZSC (Position Switch Closed) and ZSO (Position Switch Open) are universally used in industry because they are more explicit and self-documenting. Most engineers consider them effectively standard.
zsc_zso_clarification.both_valid: The system should accept BOTH conventions: ZSH/ZSL (strict ISA) and ZSC/ZSO (industry standard). Neither is wrong.
zsc_zso_clarification.do_not_flag: Never flag ZSC or ZSO as errors. Never flag ZSH or ZSL as errors. Both are correct.

loop_composition_source_note.description: ISA 5.1 does NOT define loop composition rules. It only defines how to identify individual instruments. Loop composition templates in this file are industry practice — near-universal but not formally standardized.
loop_composition_source_note.implication: When flagging missing instruments in a loop, always mark as INFORMATIONAL, never as ERROR. A loop without an FY (relay) is not wrong — it might use a direct DCS output.

validation_priorities.description: What to check first, in order of impact on data quality
order: 1. OCR character substitution in TYPE field (digits where letters should be)
2. Prefix consistency across the same P&ID drawing
3. Loop number format consistency (all numeric, consistent digit count)
6. Duplicate fullTag values (exact duplicates = likely missed detection)
7. Tag type validity against ISA function letters
8. Missing components (empty prefix, type, loop, or suffix)
